title: Neomammillaria vaupelii
intro: Neomammillaria vaupelii, also known as the Vaupel's pincushion cactus, is a species of flowering cactus native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria vaupelii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its potential health benefits and treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria vaupelii is now classified as Mammillaria vaupelii. It belongs to the family Cactaceae. The genus Mammillaria is a group of small, globular to cylindrical cacti, often referred to as "bird's nest cacti." Mammillaria vaupelii is native to Mexico, specifically in the states of Chiapas and Oaxaca. This species is part of the subgenus Mammillaria subgenus Mammillaria.
uses: Neomammillaria vaupelii, also known as Vaupel's pincushion cactus, has been traditionally used in folk medicine.
